## Cases mentioned in passing

### New York City titanium heat exchanger corrosion failure
- **canonical_cluster_id:** "New York City titanium heat exchanger corrosion failure"
- **Anchor:** `§6.p2`
- **Quote (layer 3):** "I had a case where some guys in New York City, just after 9/11, in another skyscraper, had a titanium heat exchanger. You can corrode titanium. In this case they got some silt — someone opened the wrong valve, and they got all the East River silt into their heat exchanger, which is a real problem. You get what we call under-deposit corrosion attack, and titanium can corrode under some of those conditions, and it did. They had to replace this heat exchanger. They hired immigrants who couldn't speak English, because they were the only ones who would just do this and wouldn't ask questions. They..."
